## One-Line Fibonacci Function

```python
fib = lambda n: n if n <= 1 else fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2)
```

### Usage
```python
print(fib(0))  # 0
print(fib(1))  # 1
print(fib(6))  # 8
print(fib(10)) # 55
```

### More Efficient Version (with `lru_cache`)
For larger values of `n`, use memoization to avoid redundant calculations:

```python
from functools import lru_cache

fib = lru_cache(maxsize=None)(lambda n: n if n <= 1 else fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2))
```

| Version | Time Complexity | Space Complexity |
|---------|----------------|-----------------|
| Basic   | O(2ⁿ)          | O(n)            |
| Cached  | O(n)           | O(n)            |

The cached version is recommended for any practical use, as the basic recursive version becomes very slow for `n > 35`.
